What's going on everyone? Okay, so this is a very quick video, but I want to explain this in a little bit more detail. I already talked about the different plays in the overview, but ultimately you're going to have a front-end hook and a back-end hook. What I mean by that is that the front-end hook is whatever we marketed it externally, and the back-end hook is what we're going to market to them internally to get them to stay longer on the back-end. So we're using that as bait to get them to stay and continue on their membership. So basically, so say someone signs up for a 42-day detox, right, and they're at the halfway point. What we want to do there is give them on the back-end of the basically the halfway point, it's like awesome, after you've finished the detox, we're going to put you into a six-week program to boost your metabolism and get you ready to rock and keep this momentum going. Change up the meal plan, change it, reintroduce some different foods. You don't have to eat. So the point is that we're selling different. It's just different packaging, but the thing is people need excitement and they need things to keep them going. And especially in the beginning, the first six weeks, people are just really getting their habits going, but if we can extend that to 12 weeks plus and we can push them past the 12-week barrier, that is where you can create lifetime-long customers if you have over-delivered over that first 12-week period. So if you go from six weeks straight into EFT, obviously works, we did that for years, but having a back-end offer that has as much sex appeal, and I say that tongue-in-cheek, but as much zestiness, it gives you a great offer to make them. During this, you're already selling the next thing to get them excited and then transitioning them into the next program. Mind you, whether you do a six-week challenge on the front-end or 42-day detox on the front-end, both of them are going to still have a weight loss component to them with the money-back component. The back-end, whichever one you're using on the back-end, you don't need to do any of that stuff from the weight loss money-back component at all. They should already be excited and you just say, like, hey, this is the new program, this is the program you do after this, this is what's going to recalibrate you. And it also provides an opportunity for you to explain that this is a system. This is really just step one, and you really can't just do step one. We need step two as well because we need to recalibrate you, we need to reset your metabolism, we need to get you... Basically, we don't want you to gain the weight back. And so we need to create the optimal environment so you can keep the weight off, and that's what step two is about. Okay, so hopefully that makes sense. I'll get more into the actual selling pieces of that, but I just want you to understand the overarching structure of which offer you're putting on the front-end or the back-end. It does not matter which order you're doing it in. The point is one of them is going to be what you're marketing, and the other one is going to be what you're going to be marketing to them once they are customers. And this list is not exhausted, we'll probably add more big plays to this, but this is just a starter for you to get going.
